title: Birth outcomes and prenatal exposure to ozone, carbon monoxide, and particulate matter: results from the Children's Health Study.
ehr_evidence: computerized linkage of participants with the California Department of Health Services Birth Statistical Master Files and Birth Cohort Files
summary: Study investigating the effects of ambient air pollutants (O3, CO, PM10, NO2) on birth weight and intrauterine growth retardation (IUGR) in term infants born in southern California.
key_findings: Investigated associations between birth weight, LBW, and IUGR in term newborns and air pollution data.
population: children born in California between 1975 and 1987
limitations: trimester-specific findings from previous studies are inconsistent
